data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-04-03T01:55:06.878Z</current_datetime>

I'm migrating this .NET Framework code to .NET 8 and need to ensure all Thread.Abort
usage is addressed. Check this code and provide your Thread.Abort migration guidance.

```csharp
public class SimpleWorker
{
    public void RunBatch(List<WorkItem> items)
    {
        var threads = new List<Thread>();

        foreach (var item in items)
        {
            var t = new Thread(() => Process(item));
            t.Start();
            threads.Add(t);
        }

        foreach (var t in threads)
            t.Join();
    }

    private void Process(WorkItem item)
    {
        // simulate work
        Thread.Sleep(100);
        item.Result = ComputeResult(item.Input);
    }
}
```

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about migrating Thread.Abort usage to .NET 8. Let me look at the code they provided - it uses `Thread.Start`, `Thread.Join`, and `Thread.Sleep`, but does NOT actually use `Thread.Abort`, `ThreadAbortException`, or `Thread.ResetAbort`.

The thread-abort-migration skill description says:
- USE FOR: modernizing code that calls Thread.Abort, catching ThreadAbortException, replacing Thread.ResetAbort, etc.
- DO NOT USE FOR: code that only uses Thread.Join, Thread.Sleep, or Thread.Start without any abort, interrupt, or ThreadAbortException usage


Since their code doesn't actually use Thread.Abort at all, there's no migration needed for that specific concern - it'll run fine on .NET 8 as-is. I could mention that modernizing to Task-based patterns would be a good idea, but that's a separate improvement from the abort migration they asked about.
